content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
July 3O, 199 O
Joyce B. Ladar
507 PoIk St. suite
san Francisco, CA
310
9 4]-02-3344
Re: Your Request
our File No. for Informal Advice
A 90-4L7
Dear I'Is . Ladar :
You are seeking advice on behalf of the rrNo on P, Yes on v'l
conmittee (trthe committeet') regarding the use of campaign funds to
pay legal fees under the PoLitical Reform Act (the rActtr).r
OUESTION
May a ballot neasure comrnittee use caropaign funds to pay at-
torney fees?
CONCLUSION
Canpaign funds may be used to pay attorney fees for the
committee's defense against litigation 
arising directly out of an
actj,on brought for viol,ations of campaign or disclosure laws, and
for the preparation of required caropaign disclosure forms.
FACTS
when the san Francisco District Attorney's office and the
Fair Political Practices Commission began investigations into
whether the committee had complied with disclosure requ j-rements,
the committee's treasurer, Kevin starr, retained you on behalf of
the committee.
You completed and filed the required forns and counseled the
cornmittee's officers throughout the investigations and grand jury
proceedings. You conferred s/ith the deputy district 
attorney and
his investigator as weLl as an attorney in the Fair Political
Practices comrnission enforcement division. 
Although no charges
'I- Governnent code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. Al-l- references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 ] Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804.0807 . (916)322-5660


FiLe No. A-90-417
Page 2
$rere filed against your clients, four other persons are facing
criminal charges.
From the billinq statements you enclosed with your letter, it
appears that some paynents have already been made for your
seivices, and $3,316.33 renains due. You did not state who was
the source of the payments previously made.
ANALYSIS
The conmission does not provide advice as to past conduct,
Regulation 18329(c) (4) (A), and accordingly this Letter will not
address the paynents that have already been rnade.
As to future Palments, Sections 85800 g! gc![s qrovern the use
of canpaign funas held by ballot measure conmittees. section
s58oo(;) provides that chapter 5, Article 8 of the Act applies to
ba11ot measure conmittees. The general standard applied to the
expenditure of campaign funds is that the expenditures must be
related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 85801).
Caurpaign funds may be used to pay for professional services
reasonably iequired by a cornnittee to assist it in the perfornance
of its adniniitrative functions. 
(section 85802(b) (1).) 
The
preparation and filing of disclosure forms is an administrative
iun-ction, and therefoie appears to be appropriately paid for with
carnpaign funds.
Section 85802.5 provides that campaign funds nay be used to
pay attorney's fees under limited circumstances:
Expenditures of cautpaign funds for attorney's
fees and other costs in connection with adninistra-
tive, civi1, or criminal litigation 
are not
directly related to a political, 
Iegislative, or
governnental purpose except rhere the litigation
rises directly out of a candidate's or elected
officer's activities, 
duties, or status as a
candidate or elected officer, including, but not
Iirnited to, an action to enjoin defanation, defense
of an action to enjoin defarnation, defense of an
action brouqht for a violation of state or locaI
campaiqn, disctosure, or election laws, and an ac-
tion arising fron an election contest or recount.
(Emphasis added. )
Section 85802.5 specifically refers to candidates and elected
officers, but does not nention coromittees. However' as previously
indicated, section 85800 states that the entire article on use of
canpaign funds applies to ballot measure conmittees as well as
candidates, elected officers, and other coruoittees. No other sec-
tion specifically covers use of conmittee canpaign funds to pay
attorney fees. Even if 85802.5 rrere not extended to cover ballot


File No. A-90-417
Page 3
measure committees, it rnay be used as a guide for interpreting
whether expendj.tures for attorney's fees by ba11ot measure comrnit-
tees are reasonably or directJ.y related to a political, 
legisla-
tive, or governmental purpose (Section 85802).
Whether Section 85802.5 governs use of committee funds for
paynent of attorney's fees, or $/hether that section is used merely
as a gTuide, we conclude that the funds may be used to pay attorney
fees under the facts presented. Section a52o2.5 permits
expenditures for attorney's fees when incurred due to litigation
arising directly out of a conmittee's activities, 
duties or
status, including defense of an action brought for a vioLation of
carnpaign or disclosure lasrs. Your rrork involved matters arising
directly out of the cornrnittee's activj.ties and duties, with
respect to the election, and nore inportantly, its duties and
responsibilities under the Act. T$ro Prosecutorial agencies had
initiated investigationsi the case was brought before the grand
jury; crininal charqes were filed against other persons. Under
these circumstances, we conclude that the hrork you did for your
clients appears to be sufficiently related to a political 
purpose
to pernit the expenditure of canpaign funds-
The Conmission is currently reviewing the applicability of
Section 85800 to each of the use-of -canpaign-fund sections (Sec-
tions 85800 et seq.). Accordingly, the advice given in this let-
ter is subject to change and should not be relied upon for future
questions, but only for the specific situation described in your
letter.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further guestions regarding this let-
ter please contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
s incere Iy,
scott
Ha l labr in
General counsel
SH: PB : p1h
Bernardy
Counsel, Legal
